primary_accounting_issue: Prise en compte du risque de défaut/du risque de crédit dans l’évaluation de la juste valeur d’un actif par rapport à celle d’un passif selon IFRS.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I selected IFRS 13 because, applying the precedence rule and issue focus, it is the single governing IFRS that directly defines fair value and prescribes the fair value measurement framework, including how risk assumptions and non-performance risk affect assets and liabilities.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — the question is framed as a general fair value measurement issue, and IFRS 13 directly governs that framework.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern whether and how credit or default risk is incorporated into fair value measurements of assets and liabilities.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs confirm, in a specific financial liability context, how changes attributable to a liability’s credit risk are identified and presented after fair value has been measured.
This paragraph supports the role of IFRS 13 in determining fair value on initial recognition when fair value differs from transaction price.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs address hedge accounting mechanics rather than the core fair value measurement question about incorporating default risk.
These paragraphs are only topically related through use of fair value and do not help identify peer approaches for credit/default risk in fair value measurement.

treatment_families.family: Fair value measurement of an asset using market participant assumptions about risk in expected cash flows or pricing inputs
Fair value measurement of a liability incorporating non-performance risk, including the issuer's own credit risk

approaches.label: Fair value measurement of an asset
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 13 requires fair value of an asset to reflect market participant assumptions about risk, which for a financial asset can include counterparty default or credit risk embedded in the asset’s pricing.

approaches.label: Fair value measurement of a liability
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 13 separately requires fair value of a liability to reflect non-performance risk, including the entity’s own credit standing, making liability credit risk a distinct top-level measurement approach.
